feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: 2LIS_03_BX and 2LIS_03_BF equivalent CDS views in S/4
entry.content: We were using 2LIS_03_BX and 2LIS_03_BF ODP extractor to calculate the Inventory at all the attributes level to create a analytics product in our BW analytics platform. when come to S/4 system, covering all the important fields what the equivalent CDS views?. As per SAP recommendation we need to use the CDS views for data extraction. please provide your best recommendation.

entry.title: Silent Failure in SAP Datasphere Replication Flows - A Real Production Gap Nobody Talks About
entry.content:
While working on a live SAP BDC project, I noticed a critical monitoring gap in Replication Flows that I haven't seen documented anywhere clearly.

The Problem

When a table inside a Replication Flow gets stuck in "Retrying" state during Initial and Delta load, the Replication Flow itself shows no error, no warning, no alert. It simply shows retry count — sometimes 2000+ retries — and continues silently. 

From the outside, the flow looks completely healthy. This is not specific to one source — whether you use S/4HANA, SLT, SuccessFactors or any other source, the same gap exists. You only discover the issue when you: Manually open each Replication Flow and drill into each individual table inside it and check the latest loaded timestamp manually in Data Integration Monitor.

The Scale Problem

In a real enterprise BDC project, you can easily have 300+ Replication Flows, each containing multiple tables. Nobody can realistically open every flow and check every table daily in Data Integration Monitor.

The result? I only found out a table had been silently retrying for hours when either manually checking each flow daily or the reporting/KPI team flagged that their data wasn't populating. The problem had already reached downstream before anyone on the data team knew.

My Suggestion to SAP Team

A configurable smart threshold option per Replication Flow — with two options:

Time based — if no successful load has happened in X hours since the last successful load, mark as Failed and trigger alert

Count based — if consecutive retry count since last successful load exceeds X times, mark as Failed and trigger alert

The key word here is since last successful load. In Initial and Delta load, retrying between successful loads is normal expected behavior. So, the threshold should not count total retries ever — it should reset the counter every time a table loads successfully and only trigger an alert when retries keep happening with no successful load in between.

This is a small but critical distinction that makes the feature actually usable for delta replication scenarios.

Almost every enterprise ETL tool has this capability. For a platform like BDC targeting large enterprise deployments, this monitoring gap needs to be addressed.

Has anyone else faced this? Would love to know if you found a workaround?

@SAP SAP Datasphere SAP Business Data Cloud

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA PCE Migration: Datasphere vs. BODS with Azure – Architecture Recommendations
entry.content:
Hi

My organization has begun its SAP S/4HANA PCE migration. As part of our Phase 2 roadmap, we are evaluating between SAP Datasphere and SAP BusinessObjects Data Services (BODS) with Microsoft Azure serving as the data storage layer.

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) will be our enterprise reporting and analytics solution.

FYI, we do not currently have an enterprise data warehouse.

Given this landscape, I would appreciate the community's insights and recommendations on the following topics:

  • Does BODS support extraction of SAP S/4HANA CDS views? If so, are there any limitations or best practices?
  • How well does BODS integrate with Azure-based data storage for SAP data?
  • How do the licensing costs compare between SAP Datasphere (including storage) and a BODS + Azure storage architecture?
  • What capabilities or features would we be giving up by choosing BODS with Azure storage instead of SAP Datasphere?
  • Are there any other architectural, operational, or long-term considerations that we should take into account before making this decision?

Any experiences, recommendations, or lessons learned from similar implementations would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Clarification on Using BW DataSources in Datasphere Migration
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

We are currently migrating our BW data flows to SAP Datasphere.

During this migration, we have encountered a challenge in identifying appropriate CDS views for certain objects. Some of the required fields are custom fields that were previously used in BW DSOs, and for these cases we are currently relying on BW DataSources (For Example: 0HR_PA_0, 0HR_PA_1, 0HRPOSITION_ATTR and 0EC_PCA_1). 

Can we use these Datasources or do we need to create CDS views ? Which one is preferable ?

However, we are unable to identify equivalent CDS views or the corresponding underlying tables for some of these fields.

Could you please confirm whether using BW DataSources in such scenarios is considered a recommended or acceptable best practice when equivalent CDS views are not available?

Additionally, if it is acceptable to use BW DataSources in these cases, could you also confirm whether the same approach can be applied consistently across other functional modules where similar limitations exist?

We would appreciate your guidance on this matter.

Thank you in advance for your support.

 

Regards,
Radhakrushna

entry.title: How to to consume SA) datasphere data in on prem HANA
entry.content:
Our BW functional team want to expose datasphere views by using open SQL Schema and create remote source in on premise HANA DB using ODBC .Is this feature supported by SAP datasphere if yes please provide the SAP documentation 

 

is below link is suitable to consume data sphere data in to on Prem HANA using ODBC connection

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/43509d67b8b84e66a30851e832f66911/4db6f5a329af44509ae422ad707877b2.html

entry.title: Assignment of Scoped roles in Datasphere via cloud identity services
entry.content:
Hi,
Is it possible to assign custom Scoped roles in SAP Datasphere via Cloud identity services- Identity authentication as source and SAP Datasphere as Target system?

entry.title: Unable to access Local Tables using Space Database User via JDBC/ODBC in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: We need to expose a SAP Datasphere local table through SQL access so that an external third-party application can consume the data. This is part of validating the end-to-end integration architecture where SAP S/4HANA data is replicated into SAP Datasphere, transformed (including SCD Type 2 logic), and made available for external consumption. The current local table is being used as a proof of concept before connecting to the live SAP S/4HANA source.

entry.title: Extracting CO planning data in S/4HANA for Datasphere/SAC
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

In our current project we completed a migration to SAP S/4HANA and we are now starting to use SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud for reporting (we're new with SAP reporting).

Before the migration, one of our reporting model relied on extractions from tables COSP and COSS. From what I understand, in S/4HANA the actuals are now stored in ACDOCA (type 4 and 11), and based on the available Datasphere business content I found the CDS view that allows me to extract actual cost data from ACDOCA.

However, I am unsure how to handle the planning data that previously came from COSP and COSS. Are these planning records now stored in ACDOCP in S/4HANA? Or do I need to design and develop a custom CDS view or another extraction process to retrieve planning data for COSP and COSS reporting?

Any guidance or best practices for handling COSP/COSS planning data in S/4HANA (especially when integrating with Datasphere) would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks and regards.

entry.title: Extracting COSP data in S/4HANA for Datasphere/SAC
entry.content:
Hello experts,

In our current project we completed a migration to SAP S/4HANA and we are now starting to use SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud for reporting (we're relatively new to SAP's reporting reporting tools).

Before the migration, our reporting relied on extractions from tables COSP and COSS. From what I understand, in S/4HANA the actuals are now stored in ACDOCA, and based on the available Datasphere business content I found a CDS view that allows me to extract actual data from ACDOCA.

However, I am unsure how to handle the planning data that previously came from COSP and COSS.
Are these planning records now stored in ACDOCP in S/4HANA?
Or do I need to design a custom CDS view or another extraction process to retrieve planning data for COSP reporting?

Any guidance or best practices for handling COSP/COSS planning data in S/4HANA (especially when integrating with Datasphere and SAC) would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Function YTD() not working in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi expert,

I am trying to implement the YTD restricted measure using the YTD() function, which is documented in the SAP online help

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/c8a54ee704e94e15926551293243fd1d/666fac98493f49d788b3ba72017a5012.html

However, after following the SAP online help, the YTD value is still not showing up in the data preview.

Does anyone have any suggestion? Thank you.

Data in Fact view

Fact view associated with Date dimension

 

Analytica model with YTD function on posting date:

After deploying the analytics model, the data preview didn't show any value for YTD or QTD measure.

entry.title: Wrong value in CDS View YY1_SUPDEMANDITEMS
entry.content:
I am facing a mismatch between the output of custom CDS View YY1_SUPDEMANDITEMS and T- code: MD04. 
My question is how data is loaded from T code: MD04 to cds: YY1_SUPDEMANDITEMS.

S4:   
T code: MD04  
For Material:- BSA1007208401, Plant:-4060
Here, Requiremnt:-1281  

   
S4 CdS:- YY1_SUPDEMANDITEMS 
For Material:- BSA1007208401, Plant:-4060 
mrpelement_open_quantity :-1460  

 

Sum of mrpelemnt_open_qnantity = 1460 , which is not matching with T code value.

entry.title: Pushing data from datasphere to SFTP through replication flow
entry.content:
We are pushing data from graphical view from datasphere to SFTP through replication flow and it is initial load

when we run replication flow a folder is created on SFTP path with Target table name from Replication flow, inside which Initial folder is created and file is generated with a system generated name.

We have 2 questions 
1) can we avoid creation of "Initial" Folder and place final file directly under target table folder 
2) can we give static name to final file which is placed (currently it is a system generated file name)

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud Seamless Planning – Data Retraction to S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm trying to understand the technical architecture of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Seamless Planning when it comes to writing planning data back to SAP S/4HANA.

From SAP documentation, the traditional approach is to use an SAC Export Job to write planning data from SAP Analytics Cloud to S/4HANA (e.g., ACDOCP). However, with BDC Seamless Planning, planning data is said to be physically stored in SAP Datasphere rather than duplicated in SAC.

My questions are:

  1. Is data retraction/write-back to S/4HANA officially supported with BDC Seamless Planning?

  2. If yes, what is the technical flow?

    • Does the SAC Export Job still work as before?

    • If so, where does it retrieve the planning data from—does SAC maintain a cache, or is the data read directly from Datasphere?

  3. Is there any additional configuration required compared to the traditional SAC Planning → S/4HANA export?

  4. Are there any SAP notes, official documentation, or architecture diagrams that explain this end-to-end process?

I've found documentation covering traditional SAC Planning retraction, but I haven't found any official guidance explaining how this works specifically with BDC Seamless Planning, where the planning data resides in Datasphere.

Any clarification or references would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

#BDC #Seamlessplanning #SAC #Datasphere

entry.title: Issues in delta load in SAP Datasphere from S4HANA CDS views
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

Please note that we are performing data extraction from our S/4HANA 2023 system. The data sources of our preference is CDS views. We are using Replication Flows in SAP Datasphere (by-pass) to extract data from CDS views & push it into SAP HANA Cloud DB (schema). Below are the issues we are facing:

  1. I_SLSPRCGCONDITIONRECORD is a another std CDS view we want to consume, but is NOT enable for data extraction or delta-capture. We have created a wrapper CDS view & added the below 2 annotations". The wrapper/ custom CDS view (/<namespace>/I_SLSPRCGCONDITIONRECORD) is accessible & replication flows allows to configure it for Initial and Delta load, but fails upon execution with error message: CDS view not enabled for delta extraction. Is it failing because the std. CDS view upon which wrapper was created did NOT have delta-capture annotation? The wrapper view has the required annotation, as attached.
  2. If we need to extract data from certain tables(s) in S4HANA, but the standard CDS view is NOT delta enabled. What is the suggested method for delta extraction in Datasphere?
  3. We understand that Initial and Delta replication has stricter limits on parallel jobs and number of objects compared to Initial Only due to delta tracking overhead. What are the limitations?

Thanks in advance.
Regards,
Aravind Raghavan

entry.title: Business Data Cloud & Datasphere: Are the Needs of Mid-Sized Companies Being Adequately Considered?
entry.content:
For the past one and a half years, we have been working on a migration project from a traditional SAP BW 7.5 system to SAP Datasphere. During this journey, we have experienced many positive developments. At the same time, however, we continue to encounter the same challenge: the technology often evolves faster than the associated licensing models that are economically viable for mid-sized companies.

About one and a half years ago we wanted to set up a Datasphere instance and start the migration. Since SAP Business Data Cloud had just been announced, it seemed natural not to sign a traditional Datasphere contract but instead to go directly with a BDC contract.

At that time, Business Data Cloud was still in its early stages, and many licensing details had not yet been fully clarified. As a customer, it sometimes felt as though we were among the first adopters, working alongside SAP to determine how certain licensing models could be implemented in practice from both a commercial and architectural perspective.

Today, we find ourselves facing a comparable challenge once again.

The Object Store as a Key Component

In our productive Datasphere environment, we are already using the Object Store. For several scenarios, particularly BW legacy data, data historization and future data product use cases, it represents an important building block for us. However, we currently store only around 60 GB of data there.

Looking ahead to future AI capabilities and a modern data architecture, the Object Store also appears to be a good choice. Anyone planning to build data products, AI applications, and a reliable data foundation will eventually have to address this topic.

We would now like to establish a second Datasphere instance for testing and development purposes.

This is where the challenge begins.

Based on our current understanding, the use of the Object Store is tied to a minimum configuration of 4,150 Capacity Units. While this may be reasonable for our productive environment, it is simply oversized for a small test system. For our requirements, the typical minimum configuration of approximately 1,500 Capacity Units for Datasphere would be more than sufficient.

In the test environment, we neither require years of historical data nor large storage capacities. Nevertheless, there currently seems to be no option to license the Object Store in a smaller configuration.

From an outside perspective, this appears more like a commercial limitation than a technical one. After all, SAP Data Products can also run on an Object Store. However, I am not an SAP developer; I am a Product Owner in a mid-sized company. Perhaps there are technical considerations that I am overlooking.

What Options Does a Mid-Sized Customer Have?

From our perspective, there are currently only three possible options:

1. Purchase a Significantly Larger Datasphere Test Instance Than Actually Required

This would allow us to use the Object Store, but the additional cost would be difficult to justify from a business perspective.

→ Economically not viable

2. Avoid Using the Object Store Altogether

This would prevent us from implementing certain data product and AI scenarios. In that case, we might have to wait one or two years until SAP provides a more suitable solution.

→ Not an ideal foundation for future innovation

3. Use the Object Store Only in the Productive Environment

This would introduce new challenges regarding development, testing, and transports between systems.

→ Maintainability and operational complexity would suffer significantly

None of these options appears particularly satisfactory.

I have already found an SAP Influence Request that addresses exactly this topic. However, for our current situation, it does not provide a practical solution, because it will be to late...

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#idea/378537

For mid-sized companies in particular, this creates a substantial entry barrier. Many customers want to test innovative capabilities such as Data Products, AI applications, and the Object Store, but they do not require an enterprise-scale test environment.

Is a Mid-Market Option Missing?

From a customer perspective, the question arises whether SAP is adequately considering the needs of small and mid-sized businesses in this area.

Fundamentally, we are convinced by the strategic direction of Business Data Cloud and Datasphere. However, there is an impression that certain licensing models are primarily designed for larger customers and more extensive deployment scenarios.

A more flexible option for smaller environments could remove many barriers and significantly increase the adoption of new capabilities. Especially in innovation and proof-of-concept projects, organizations need the ability to experiment with new technologies in an economically reasonable and low-risk way before scaling them further.

Open Question to the Community

Perhaps we are overlooking an option, or perhaps other customers have already found ways to address similar challenges.

  • How are you dealing with this topic?
  • Have you found alternative approaches for test and development environments?
  • Are there ways to use the Object Store more economically?
  • Or have you encountered the same limitations?

I look forward to hearing about your experiences and engaging in a constructive discussion.

Our intention is not to criticize SAP in general. On the contrary, we want to successfully adopt Business Data Cloud and Datasphere and see significant potential in these new technologies. At the moment, however, we feel that a licensing model better aligned with the realities of mid-sized companies is still missing.

Best regards,
Niels

entry.title: Error when trying to add new member to account dimension SAC/Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am facing the below issue when I am trying to add a new member to the account dimension, it was working before normally.

Anyone have faced this issue before ?

Thanks for the help

entry.title: IBP Data integration by using Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi,

We created IBP integration with Datasphere by following the below link

Access SAP IBP Master & Key Figures data using ODa... - SAP Community

and we are successfully created the connection in Datasphere and created data flow

But when loading it throwing error as below - Please find attached document for your refrence

HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request{"error":{"code":"/IBP/CX_BO_MDEO","message":{"lang":"en","value":"Request cannot be processed; check the planning area and version values"},"innererror":{"application":{"component_id":"SCM-IBP-INT-ODT","service_namespace":"/IBP/","service_id":"MASTER_DATA_API_SRV","service_version":"0001"},"transactionid":"6DFFBDC8ADC501E0E006A5A72845BF3D","timestamp":"20260717182053.5310810","Error_Resolution":{"SAP_Transaction":"For backend administrators: use ADT feed reader \"SAP Gateway Error Log\" or run transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on SAP Gateway hub system and searc

entry.title: Remote Tables connectivity directly with CDS entities with cloud connector
entry.content: Remote Tables connectivity directly with CDS entities with cloud connector (With out service Binding -> SQL Binding type)

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Replication Flow Delta Run Fails: “Space Table Is Used by One or More Tasks”
entry.content:
Hi, 

We are facing an issue with SAP Datasphere Replication Flows where the initial load completed successfully, but a subsequent delta run failed.

Landscape

We have:

  • Two different SAP Datasphere Spaces
  • One Replication Flow in each Space
  • Both Replication Flows consume the same S/4HANA CDS extraction view
  • Both flows write data to the same ADLS target location
  • The Replication Flow created recently in the new Space is running successfully
  • The older Replication Flow in the original Space completed earlier runs successfully but is now failing during the delta run
  • Its not included into any Task Chains either

Error:

Replication dataset re-creation failed with error: space table ZCDS_TCURC_DEX_CDC is used by one or more tasks.
We have more failed RFs with ran initially but later fails for delta but runs fine in the newly created space

Thanks & Regards,
Sangey.

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Remote Tables connectivity directly with CDS entities with cloud connector
